did.head: DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY
did.repository: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library
did.abstract: Professional papers include correspondence, subject files, and files relating to his work with the Science, Technology, and Society program at Cornell. Also, manuscript of Black's book Rational Choice, the Prisoner's Dilemma.

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Philosopher, Professor of Philosophy at Cornell University.

Max Black received two doctoral degrees from the University of London. He taught at the University of London from 1936 to 1940 and at the University of Illinois from 1940 to 1946. He joined the faculty at Cornell in 1946, becoming the Susan Linn Sage Professor of Philosophy in 1954. In 1965, Black became the first director of Cornell's Society for the Humanities, a position he held until 1971. Since 1971, he was a member of the Cornell program on Science, Technology and Society and academic head of its Unit on Humanities, Science and Technology. His first publication was a discussion of Jeremy Bentham's theory of definition. He has written numerous works on philosophical subjects, including books on mathematics, Wittgenstein, and the nature of the university.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Professional papers include correspondence, subject files, and files relating to his work with the Science, Technology, and Society program at Cornell. Also, manuscript of Black's book Rational Choice, the Prisoner's Dilemma.

did.unittitle: Probability; Induction; Mss. (Past); Cornell University matters; Fellowships & Grants; Lectures; Publications, Society for Humanities
Papers; Testimonials; reprints of his writings
Course files; Paper on Necessity; Alphabetical file A-Z; Notes A-P on various philosophical subjects
Notes P-W on various philosophical subjects; Tractatus by Wittgenstein (notes and papers)
Wittgenstein continued; Writing Projects (notes, course notes, arranged alphabetically A-S); Social Science (course notes, reprints, etc.)
Course work, notes, drafts, papers, reprints on philosophical topics
Course work for systematics; Philosophy of Science course material; India (1962); Japan (1957); Philosophy of Education
Reprints by others; Topics (notes and papers); Seminars (Past); Tarners Lecture
Bound typescript copies of papers by others; Logical Possibility by M.B. (bd. typsc.) - 2 cassettes
Alphabetical Subject Files; Misc. I & J, Knowledge, Language, Literary Criticism, Logic, Measurement, Metaphors, Moore and "O"
Subject Files continued, O-R: Observation, Paradoxes, Philosophy, Practical Reasoning, Probability, Rationality, Reasonable Rules, Russell
Subject Files continued, S-W: Scholarly Responsibility, Sets, Should and Is, Technion Papers, Technology, University (Open), Verifiability
Wittgenstein continued; Misc. in separate drawer, incl. Lord Ashby, Humbug
Professional alpha file A-W incls i.e. C.U. Adult Univ.; Financial
Subject files; reprints of M. Black; India
Typescripts Memoir of M.B. (Rosenbaum, interviewer); cassettes of lectures; notes
Notes on philosophical subjects: incl. Tarners Lecture series
Alpha file: M-Skepticism, Science, Spare, "P"; course material
Course material; Other Papers; Dromoland Papers, 1972
Subject files, A-I: Aspen Colloquium, Communication; Determinism; Explanation; Goodman; Humanism; Humanities, Induction
Humanities Science and Technology - Rockefeller Foundation, Correspondence and Proposals, Annual Reports
Humanities Science and Technology - Annual Reports, Colloquia, Workshops
NEH and NSF - Proposals, Grants and Correspondence
Max Black's Memoir, including original tape recordings from oral interview
Letters to Rosenbaum and Naomi Black

scopecontent.p: With a few replies
Includes expense logs; notes; doodles

did.unittitle: A Companion to Wittgenstein's 'Tractatus' by Max Black

scopecontent.p: Written in examination books
Correspondence
Includes medicine bills, vaccination record, passports, correspondence, death certificates
Includes bills, receipts, permission letters, publishing letters
Contains letter, infant lock of hair, copy of text, Mind Association members' addresses; letter from Mrs. Ashton Edwards; "philosopher + fish" quote
Includes "alien registration receipt card," registration receipts, immigrant identification cards, identity cards, correspondence
Includes marriage registration, birth certificates, certificate of naturalization
Correspondence; newspaper clipping of obituary
Includes certificate of naturalization, an oath of allegiance

did.unittitle: Willie (and Sylina) - Their Daughter, Elisabeth Halpern - Correspondence

scopecontent.p: Research papers
Contains argument for 'Perplexities,' text of "The 'Factual' and the 'Normative',"
Includes correspondence, bank notes, material related to King Alfred School
Includes small notebook of Japanese phrases, daily logs, expense logs, dresses, passport info; envelope of newspaper clippings
Includes correspondence and letters of recommendation
Includes photo of Christopher + Felicity Gittenden (?) when they were children
Correspondence; book lists; handwritten notes; flyer for "Special University Lectures in Philosophy" at the University of London
One letter
One letter
Correspondence

Correspondence; copy of Life with Father, Life with Socrates by Virginia Wetherbee; copy of Are There Any Philosophically Interesting Questions in Technology?; includes two letters from Vicky, one is written on a Harvard College Library transaction card
Correspondence; packet titled "Hints for Life in Jerusalem"
Correspondence; draft of foreword of text

did.unittitle: Possible Posthumous Collection; Other "Lecturing and executive" staff

scopecontent.p: Includes postcards, rent account summaries, loan and savings books; original envelope labeled "Odds and ends"
Includes undated note
Includes correspondence from the National Endowment for the Humanities; photo negatives
A photo of Ludwig Wittgenstein
Photo negatives

Includes pages from Picture Post on various philosophy professors, newspaper clipping of philosopher Bertrand Russell, pictures of Ludwig Wittgenstein, Albert Einstein, and Emir Abdullah, a Mother's Day card (?)

did.unittitle: Dialectica-International review of philosophy of knowledge

scopecontent.p: Vol. 44, memorial issue

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 31 and 32 are restricted until the death of the donors or by their permission.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Max Black papers, #14-21-2466. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
